name: Hunt, Joshua
resolved_employer: Hunt Companies, Inc. (Executive Vice President & Vice Chairman)
evidence: Joshua W. 'Josh' Hunt is Executive Vice President and Vice Chairman of Hunt Companies, Inc., the El Paso-headquartered real estate, infrastructure and financial-services holding company founded by his family; he sits on its board and its Investment and Executive Committees. He is also president of the Woody and Gayle Hunt Family Foundation and chairman of MountainStar Sports Group (El Paso Chihuahuas, El Paso Locomotive FC). Matches the self-reported 'Executive / Hunt Companies, Inc.' and the El Paso 79902 address.

affiliations.org: Hunt Military Communities (division of Hunt Companies, Inc.)
affiliations.role: Not a personal title — but as EVP/Vice Chairman and board member of the parent company he sits atop a business whose military-housing division holds an ownership interest in 37,768 military homes in 20 states and manages 15,640 of them, making it the largest provider of military housing in the U.S. under the Department of Defense's Military Housing Privatization Initiative (MHPI). This is privatized military family housing under DoD contract, not weapons/systems contracting.
Chairman; control person for the El Paso Chihuahuas (Triple-A affiliate of the San Diego Padres) and El Paso Locomotive FC; USL Board of Governors

affiliations.org: Woody and Gayle Hunt Family Foundation
Texas Tribune; Texas State History Museum Foundation; Trellis Foundation (Vice Chairman); Borderplex Alliance; WestStar Bank; Texas Business Leadership Council

name: Whitworth, Lisle
resolved_employer: None found — Trinity Episcopal School (Austin) board chair is an unpaid trustee role, not employment
evidence: Identity is corroborated: a 'Lisle Whitworth' is listed on the Board of Trustees of Trinity Episcopal School in Austin, matching the self-reported 'Board Chair / Trinity Episcopal School', and Lisle and Scott Whitworth appear at a 2607 Jarratt Ave address in the 78703 zip that matches this donor. However, the reported 'employer' is a volunteer school board seat, not a job, and no occupation or career for her could be established from any public source. Her husband, Gary Scott Whitworth, is founder and managing partner of WM Capital Partners in Austin, but a spouse's employer is not a basis for classifying her. Not classified rather than label her Education off an unpaid trustee role.

affiliations.role: Member, Board of Trustees (self-reports as Board Chair)
Listed with Scott Whitworth on the foundation's team page; specific role not stated on the page

name: Partin, Sara
resolved_employer: AutoWeb, Inc. (Chief Human Resources Officer / Chief People Officer)
evidence: Sara E. Partin was appointed Chief Human Resources Officer of AutoWeb, Inc. effective October 2018 and is listed as its Chief People Officer; AutoWeb describes itself as providing 'consumer leads, clicks and associated marketing services to automotive dealers and manufacturers' plus online automotive content — a digital automotive marketing/lead-generation and content business, hence Media rather than Retail or Technology. Her Atlanta-area 30319 (Brookhaven) address is corroborated by her prior Atlanta-based roles as CHRO of Yellow Pages and HR lead for Cox Automotive Media Solutions Group (Autotrader, Kelley Blue Book, Dealer.com); she also gave to Watson on the same day (2024-05-06) as Mark Greatrex, the Atlanta-based president of Cox Communications. Harvard JD, Stanford BA.

name: Chaudhari, Baldev
resolved_employer: SMEC, Inc. (Cedar Park, TX) — wholesale distributor of surface-mount electronic components; also associated with Austin-area hotel ownership
evidence: SMEC, Inc. of Cedar Park (est. 1983) is a small wholesale distributor of surface-mount electronic components — chip capacitors, chip resistors, tantalum capacitors, chip inductors, ferrite beads and filters — and its federal vendor registration lists Baldev Chaudhari as the company contact (title 'Marketing Manager'). He self-reports as 'Business Owner', which is consistent with the family-run profile (Marie and Rupal Chaudhari also appear at SMEC), though no source confirms his ownership stake outright. Public records place a Baldev Chaudhari in the Leander/Cedar Park area adjacent to his 78729 NW Austin donor zip, and also tie him to Pecan Park Hospitality LLC and Hampton Inn / Homewood Suites properties. Classified on the SMEC electronics business he reported; the title and the hotel side of his holdings keep this at medium.

name: Greene, Benjamin
resolved_employer: Traxporta by Traxión (COO); previously founder & CEO of Strive Logistics; principal of Greene Capital Partners, LLC
evidence: Ben Greene is an Austin-based logistics executive who founded and ran Strive Logistics, an Austin freight brokerage, as CEO from 2003 to 2019 until its acquisition by Redwood Logistics (a CI Capital portfolio company), joining Redwood with his management team. He is now COO of Traxporta by Traxión, and his own vehicle Greene Capital Partners, LLC appears in his work history — both self-reported employers ('Greene Capital Partners, Traxion') match, as does the Austin 78703 address. His skill set is listed as 3PL, transportation, LTL, logistics and container.

name: Morris-Blake, Barbara
evidence: Barbara Morris-Blake is the founder and CEO of Elevate Organizations, an Austin-based leadership-development and organizational consultancy she formed in 2011 that does executive coaching, culture transformation and talent work; she is a certified professional coach with an MBA in leadership and a former executive search consultant. Matches the self-reported 'Organizational Development / Elevate Organizations, LLC' and the Austin 78751 address.

name: Greatrex, Mark
evidence: Mark Greatrex was named president of Cox Communications in 2022, succeeding Pat Esser; Cox Communications is the largest private broadband company in the U.S., operating fiber networks in more than 30 states serving nearly seven million homes and businesses. He joined Cox in 2011 as chief marketing and sales officer after serving as SVP of Global Still Beverages at The Coca-Cola Company, and is a long-time Atlanta resident — matching the self-reported 'President / Cox Communications Inc.' and the Atlanta 30327 address. LSE economics degree, NYU Stern MBA.

name: Tenney, Sean
resolved_employer: Parsons Corporation — Texas regional manager, Infrastructure North America
evidence: A Sean Tenney is quoted in a June 2025 Parsons press release as 'Texas regional manager for Parsons' Infrastructure North America division', announcing Parsons' selection for the Austin-Bergstrom International Airport program management contract — an Austin-facing role that fits the self-reported 'Engineer / Parsons' and the Austin 78703 address. Held at medium because his residence could not be independently confirmed and at least one other Sean Tenney (Tri-Com Consulting Group) exists; the Austin-plus-Parsons combination is the corroboration.

affiliations.role: Texas regional manager, Infrastructure North America. Parsons describes itself as 'a leading disruptive technology provider in the national security and global infrastructure markets' with capabilities spanning cyber and intelligence and space and missile defense alongside transportation and urban development; it is a major U.S. defense and intelligence contractor. Note his role sits in the civil Infrastructure segment (airports, transportation), not the national-security segment — the defense connection is his employer's, not his function.

name: Singer, Adam
resolved_employer: ExxonMobil Technology and Engineering — Senior Principal AI Architect and Machine Learning Engineer (formerly computational scientist, Upstream Research)
evidence: Adam Singer is a computational scientist at ExxonMobil in the greater Houston area, currently Senior Principal AI Architect and Machine Learning Engineer at ExxonMobil Technology and Engineering, previously a team lead and research specialist in Upstream Research and an adjunct assistant professor of computational science at Rice; he holds a PhD in chemical engineering from MIT. The self-reported 'Computational Scientist / Exxon' matches exactly, and his 77382 (The Woodlands/Spring) address sits beside ExxonMobil's Spring campus.

affiliations.role: Technical staff — Senior Principal AI Architect / Machine Learning Engineer, formerly Upstream Research team lead; has spoken at Machine Learning in Oil & Gas and Edge Computing in Oil & Gas industry conferences. Recorded as an oil & gas connection by employment; he is not an executive, board member or owner.

name: Speers, Kelsey
resolved_employer: Democracy Forward (Senior Engagement and Education Advisor; later Interim Director, Oversight & Engagement)
evidence: Kelsey Woerner Speers, MPP, joined Democracy Forward — a Washington, DC progressive legal advocacy organization — in December 2023 as Senior Engagement and Education Advisor, and later served as Interim Director of Oversight & Engagement. She came from American Oversight, where she was Research Director, with more than a decade in government and nonprofit work; Georgetown McCourt MPP and Dartmouth BA. Matches both self-reported employers ('Democracy Forward' and 'DFF', i.e. Democracy Forward Foundation), the advisor title, the DC 20036 address, and a first gift dated the same month she was announced.

affiliations.role: Former Director of Research (also Deputy Director, Senior Investigative Researcher) — government transparency and election integrity watchdog

name: Goff McMillan, Beth
evidence: Beth Goff-McMillan is President & CEO of SKG in Austin, one of the largest MillerKnoll furniture dealerships in the country, providing workplace consulting, design, furniture, architectural and technology solutions; it is one of the largest woman-owned businesses in Austin and a three-time Inc. 5000 company. She was named Austin Business Journal's 2023 CEO of the Year and a 2026 Enterprising Women of the Year winner, and in 2021 launched FOLIO, a SaaS product for the commercial furnishings industry. Matches the self-reported 'Chief Executive Officer / SKG' and an Austin-metro 78738 address. Classified Retail as a commercial furniture dealership, though the consulting/design side of the business is substantial.

name: Maxwell, Nyle
evidence: Nyle Maxwell is CEO of the Nyle Maxwell Family of Dealerships, six Chrysler Dodge Jeep Ram and Fiat dealerships in the Austin area plus Castroville and Killeen, with 37 years in the automotive industry; he also serves on the Stellantis National Dealer Council. He was mayor of Round Rock from 2002 to 2007 and now lives in Georgetown, matching the self-reported title/employer and the Georgetown 78628 address. UT graduate.

affiliations.role: President; member since 2009 and on the Executive Committee for five years. TWA is a landowner/hunting-heritage conservation and advocacy group — not a firearms-industry or gun-lobby organization, but noted here because his bio also records that he owns multiple ranches running trophy whitetail, quail, turkey and exotic hunting operations and has earned the Texas Slam and the Grand Slam of North American Wild Sheep.

affiliations.org: Greater Round Rock Community Foundation (founder and chair, $10M+ in grants); Dell Children's Medical Center Foundation (founding member); Ascension Seton Williamson Foundation (board member)
